sections.planetterrian_spotlight:
### Planetterrian Spotlight
The shingles vaccine’s potential to slow biological aging, as reported in Science Daily, is a surprising twist on a familiar shot. Beyond preventing painful rashes, it appears to dial down chronic inflammation—a key driver of age-related issues like heart disease and frailty—in over 3,800 older Americans studied. This hints at broader protective effects, possibly benefiting millions at risk of "inflammaging." Researchers now aim to uncover how these anti-aging mechani

sections.planetterrian_spotlight:
### Planetterrian Spotlight
The stem cell infusion for frailty stands out as a direct hit on aging's toll. By targeting inflammation and tissue repair at a cellular level, it not only improved physical function in trials but could extend healthy years for the elderly, reducing reliance on drugs or intense exercise. Researchers are now eyeing larger studies to confirm safety and longevity benefits. What age-related condition would you most want stem cell therapies to tackle?
